preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatal glide [j]) into the alveolar nasal [n] is a form of nasalization and fortition, documented in specific phonetic environments where glides interact with nasal features or strengthen into consonants.

examples.note: Phonological alternation where the palatal glide corresponds to a nasal consonant, likely due to nasalization spread or coda strengthening.
Documentation of palatal glides surfacing as nasals in specific prefixal environments (e.g., in LuGanda or related processes).
